title: quercus petraea subsp. austrotyrrhenica
intro: Quercus petraea subsp. austrotyrrhenica, also known as the Dalmatian oak, is a subspecies of the sessile oak (Quercus petraea) native to the Dinaric Alps region in southeastern Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, derived from its bark, leaves, and acorns. The health benefits of Quercus petraea subsp. austrotyrrhenica include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and act as an antioxidant, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its bark can be used to make a traditional medicine called "berberis" or "dalmatian oak bark", which is believed to have a range of health benefits.
